$comment: Offline dependency manifest for company-PC deployment. status: required|planned|optional|not_needed. Prefetch everything with status=required/planned while internet is available; verify SHA256 after transfer. No secrets belong in this file or in the bundle.
runtime_note: agent_ops is stdlib-only by design: no pip wheels are required for the core runtime. Keep it that way when patching.

dependencies.name: python-embeddable
dependencies.why: Core runtime for agent_ops, diagnostics, install/repair scripts. Company PC python default must not be assumed.
dependencies.offline_install: unzip to %USERPROFILE%\OpenCodeLIG\python311\; no registry changes needed
dependencies.fallback: company-installed Python 3.11 via py -3.11 if present

dependencies.name: opencode-binary
dependencies.version: PENDING: pin exact version after local runtime validation
dependencies.source: official OpenCode release channel (verify licensing/redistribution before bundling)
dependencies.why: Primary agent runtime on the company PC.
dependencies.offline_install: copy binary; record version in diagnostics (opencode --version)

dependencies.name: python-wheels
dependencies.why: Core agent_ops runtime is stdlib-only; no wheels needed today.
dependencies.offline_verify: py -3.11 tests run with no pip packages installed

dependencies.name: chromedriver
dependencies.version: match company Chrome major version (home Chrome: check at prefetch time)
dependencies.why: Browser automation via Selenium/CDP, only if that route is chosen (doctor.py already probes CHROMEDRIVER_PATH and CDP port 9222).
dependencies.fallback: CDP via Chrome --remote-debugging-port=9222 without a driver binary

dependencies.name: pywinauto-and-deps
dependencies.why: Windows UI automation, only if that capability is adopted.
dependencies.fallback: skip UI automation; core file/LLM workflow unaffected

dependencies.name: nodejs-runtime
dependencies.version: n/a unless OpenCode distribution requires it
dependencies.why: Only if the pinned OpenCode build needs Node.

dependencies.name: office-doc-wheels
dependencies.version: PENDING_DECISION (python-pptx, openpyxl latest stable at prefetch)
dependencies.why: Direct .pptx/.xlsx generation without Office COM. Capability framework currently ships stdlib-only scaffolds (slide_outline.md/slide_spec.json/.csv/.bas); these wheels upgrade that to real files.
dependencies.offline_install: py -3.11 -m pip install --no-index --find-links release/vendor/wheels-office python-pptx openpyxl
dependencies.fallback: stdlib scaffolds + manual transfer into PowerPoint/Excel (already validated locally)

dependencies.name: pywin32
dependencies.why: COM control of Excel/Word/PowerPoint/SolidWorks/HWP, only if direct app automation is adopted. VBA macro scaffolds work without it.
dependencies.fallback: generated .bas macros imported/run inside the app manually (app validation pending)

dependencies.name: browser-automation-wheels
dependencies.version: PENDING_DECISION (selenium or playwright — pick one after company policy check)
dependencies.why: Real browser control for web/mail assistant workflows. Current browser_script scaffolds run on stdlib urllib only.
dependencies.fallback: Chrome CDP via --remote-debugging-port=9222 (no wheel needed), or procedure documents

dependencies.name: 7zip-portable
dependencies.why: Bundle extraction/verification convenience on the company PC.

transfer.format: transparent ZIP or plain folder tree; no self-extracting executables, no encoded payloads
transfer.checksum_procedure: generate release/manifest-sha256.txt via certutil -hashfile or Python hashlib; re-verify after transfer on the company PC
transfer.secret_policy: bundle must contain no lig-api.env, no API keys, no internal hostnames; verify with a pre-zip scan
